panels.description: Database uptime since postmaster start.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, extract(epoch from (now() - pg_postmaster_start_time()))::bigint as value;

panels.title: Uptime (seconds)

panels.description:
Shows the percentage of reads served from PostgreSQL’s buffer cache (memory) instead of disk.

Higher (>99%) is good; low values mean queries are hitting disk too often.

targets.rawSql:
select now() as time,
  (blks_hit::float / nullif(blks_hit + blks_read, 0)) * 100 as value
from pg_stat_database
where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Cache Hit Ratio (%)

panels.description:
Transactions per second handled by the database.

Good for measuring workload throughput.

targets.rawSql:
select now() as time,
  ( (xact_commit + xact_rollback) / greatest(extract(epoch from now() - stats_reset), 1) )::numeric(12,2) as value
from pg_stat_database
where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Estimated TPS (since last reset)

panels.description:
Number of times queries deadlocked.

Deadlocks = conflicting transactions waiting on each other. Should be near zero.
panels.title: Deadlocks (total since reset)

panels.description:
Amount of temporary space used for sorting, hashing, or large query operations.

Large values may indicate missing indexes or poorly optimized queries.
panels.title: Temp Bytes (since reset)

panels.description: Current active connections in this database (snapshot).

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, count(*)::bigint as value from pg_stat_activity where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Active Connections

panels.description: Configured maximum concurrent connections allowed by the server.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, setting::bigint as value from pg_settings where name = 'max_connections';

panels.title: Max Connections

panels.description:
Largest tables in the database (by storage size).

Useful to track data growth and detect bloated tables.

targets.rawSql:
select s.schemaname, s.relname,
       pg_total_relation_size(s.relid) as size_bytes,
       pg_total_relation_size(s.relid)::text as size_pretty
from pg_catalog.pg_statio_user_tables s
where s.schemaname = '${schema}'
order by 3 desc
limit 10;

panels.title: Top 10 Tables by Size

panels.description:
Tables scanned without using indexes.

High sequential scans on large tables = possible missing indexes or query tuning needed.

targets.rawSql:
select relname, seq_scan, idx_scan, n_live_tup as live_rows
from pg_stat_user_tables
where schemaname = '${schema}'
order by seq_scan desc
limit 10;

panels.title: Top 10 Tables by Sequential Scans

panels.description: Buffers written by backends (client backends) since reset.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, buffers_backend::bigint as value from pg_stat_bgwriter;

panels.title: Buffers backend (since reset)

panels.description: Max replication lag in bytes across standbys (on primary only).

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, coalesce(max(pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), replay_lsn)),0)::bigint as value from pg_stat_replication;

panels.title: Replication max lag (bytes)

panels.description:
Shows how many client connections are in each state (active, idle, idle in transaction).

Helps find bottlenecks from too many idle or stuck connections.

targets.rawSql:
select coalesce(state, 'unknown') as state, count(*)::bigint as count
from pg_stat_activity
where datname = current_database()
group by 1
order by 2 desc;

panels.title: Connections by State

targets.rawSql:
select s.schemaname, s.relname, s.indexrelname, s.idx_scan, s.idx_tup_read, s.idx_tup_fetch
from pg_stat_user_indexes s
join pg_index i on i.indexrelid = s.indexrelid
where s.schemaname = '${schema}'
order by s.idx_scan desc
limit 10;

panels.description:
Displays current locks by type (row-level, relation, etc.).

High lock counts can cause blocking queries or slow performance.

targets.rawSql: select locktype, count(*)::bigint as count from pg_locks group by 1 order by 2 desc;

panels.title: Locks by Type

panels.description:
Percentage of index lookups satisfied without hitting disk.

High ratio (>99%) means queries are using indexes efficiently.
panels.title: Index Hit Ratio (%)

panels.description:
Percentage of table reads served from memory.

Complements cache hit ratio; low numbers may mean memory is insufficient.
panels.title: Table Hit Ratio (%)

panels.description:
Number of checkpoints (write-all-to-disk events).

Too frequent checkpoints = high I/O, too rare = risk of long recovery times.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, (checkpoints_timed + checkpoints_req) as value from pg_stat_bgwriter;

panels.title: Checkpoints (timed + requested)

panels.description:
Number of dirty pages flushed to disk at checkpoint.

Large numbers show checkpoint I/O load.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, buffers_checkpoint as value from pg_stat_bgwriter;

panels.title: Buffers Written by Checkpoints

panels.description: Buffers cleaned by background writer since reset.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, buffers_clean::bigint as value from pg_stat_bgwriter;

panels.title: Buffers clean (since reset)

panels.description: Database size sampled over time using pg_database_size(current_database()).

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, pg_database_size(current_database())::bigint as value;

panels.title: Database size

panels.description:
Number of temporary files created.

Too many = queries using disk sorting instead of memory.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, temp_files::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Temp Files (since reset)

panels.description:
Percentage of transactions rolled back vs committed.

High rollback ratio may indicate app errors or bad query logic.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, (xact_rollback::float / nullif(xact_commit + xact_rollback, 0)) * 100 as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Rollback Ratio (%)

panels.description: Oldest active transaction age (only non-idle). High values may indicate long-running transactions blocking VACUUM.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, coalesce(extract(epoch from max(now()-xact_start))::bigint, 0) as value from pg_stat_activity where datname = current_database() and state <> 'idle' and xact_start is not null;

panels.title: Oldest active transaction age (s)

panels.description:
This panel lists the tables with the highest number of dead tuples (obsolete row versions created by UPDATE and DELETE operations). Dead tuples take up space, contribute to table bloat, and can slow down queries if not cleaned up. Normally, PostgreSQL’s autovacuum process reclaims this space, but a high dead tuple count may indicate:

Inefficient autovacuum settings

Long-running transactions blocking cleanup

Heavy update/delete activity on a table

Use this panel to identify bloated tables that may require tuning, manual vacuuming, or schema adjustments (partitioning, indexing).

targets.rawSql:
select schemaname, relname, n_live_tup, n_dead_tup, last_vacuum, last_autovacuum
from pg_stat_user_tables
where schemaname = '${schema}'
order by n_dead_tup desc
limit 10;

panels.title: Top Tables by Dead Tuples

panels.description: Deadlocks and conflicts since last stats reset. Consider alerting if non-zero growth is observed.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, deadlocks::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();
select now() as time, conflicts::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Deadlocks and conflicts

panels.description: Current active sessions per role from pg_stat_activity.

targets.rawSql: select usename as role, count(*)::bigint as active_sessions from pg_stat_activity where datname = current_database() and state = 'active' group by usename order by active_sessions desc;

panels.title: Active sessions per role

panels.description: List of superuser roles. Use with care: superusers bypass all permission checks.

targets.rawSql: select rolname, rolsuper, rolcreaterole, rolcreatedb, rolreplication from pg_roles where rolsuper = true order by rolname;

panels.title: Superuser roles

panels.description: Connections sampled over time. Uses pg_stat_database.numbackends at each refresh interval (snapshot-based).

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, numbackends::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Connections over time

panels.description: Role membership mapping (role → members).

targets.rawSql: select r.rolname as role, m.rolname as member, a.admin_option as is_admin from pg_auth_members a join pg_roles r on r.oid = a.roleid join pg_roles m on m.oid = a.member order by role, member;

panels.title: Role membership mapping

panels.description: Cumulative transactions (commits + rollbacks). Grafana can derive a rate (TPS) via Transformations → Difference/Derivative.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, (xact_commit + xact_rollback)::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Transactions (cumulative)

panels.description: Time spent reading/writing data in ms (requires track_io_timing=on).

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, blk_read_time as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();
select now() as time, blk_write_time as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();

panels.title: I/O timing (ms)

panels.description: Tuple operations (returned, fetched, inserted, updated, deleted) cumulative since reset.

targets.rawSql: select now() as time, tup_returned::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();
select now() as time, tup_fetched::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();
select now() as time, tup_inserted::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();
select now() as time, tup_updated::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();
select now() as time, tup_deleted::bigint as value from pg_stat_database where datname = current_database();

panels.title: Tuple operations (cumulative)

list.label: Schema
list.name: schema
list.query: select nspname as schema from pg_namespace where nspname not like 'pg_%' and nspname <> 'information_schema' order by 1

list.label: Table
list.name: table
list.query: select relname as table from pg_class c join pg_namespace n on n.oid = c.relnamespace where n.nspname = '${schema}' and c.relkind = 'r' order by 1
